question: Total number of customers in the warehouse
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM customers;

question: How many branches are in the North region?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM branches WHERE region = 'North';

question: Number of dormant accounts
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM accounts WHERE status = 'dormant';

question: How many active credit cards do we have?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM credit_cards WHERE status = 'active';

question: How many loans are currently overdue?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM loans WHERE status = 'overdue';

question: How many female customers do we have?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers WHERE gender = 'female';

question: Count of customers flagged with default
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers WHERE has_default = TRUE;

question: Number of products of type home_loan
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM products WHERE product_type = 'home_loan';

question: How many employees work as Loan Officers?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM employees WHERE role = 'Loan Officer';

question: Total transactions recorded
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM transactions;

question: Total balance held across all active accounts
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(current_balance) AS total FROM accounts WHERE status = 'active';

question: Average annual income of customers
gold_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(annual_income), 2) AS avg_income FROM customers;

question: Maximum credit card limit issued
gold_sql: SELECT MAX(credit_limit) AS max_limit FROM credit_cards;

question: Average loan principal across all loans
gold_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(principal), 2) AS avg_principal FROM loans;

question: Total outstanding credit card balance
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(current_balance) AS total FROM credit_cards WHERE status = 'active';

question: Number of customers by marital status
gold_sql: SELECT marital_status, COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers GROUP BY marital_status ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Number of accounts by status
gold_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS n FROM accounts GROUP BY status ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Number of customers by gender
gold_sql: SELECT gender, COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers GROUP BY gender ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Branches grouped by region
gold_sql: SELECT region, COUNT(*) AS n FROM branches GROUP BY region ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Number of customers in each state
gold_sql: SELECT state, COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers GROUP BY state ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Average customer age by education level
gold_sql: SELECT education, ROUND(AVG(age), 1) AS avg_age FROM customers GROUP BY education ORDER BY avg_age DESC;

question: Total loan principal by loan status
gold_sql: SELECT status, SUM(principal) AS total_principal FROM loans GROUP BY status ORDER BY total_principal DESC;

question: Average salary by employee role
gold_sql: SELECT role, ROUND(AVG(salary), 2) AS avg_salary FROM employees GROUP BY role ORDER BY avg_salary DESC;

question: Number of customers by preferred contact method
gold_sql: SELECT preferred_contact, COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers GROUP BY preferred_contact ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Number of transactions per channel
gold_sql: SELECT channel, COUNT(*) AS n FROM transactions GROUP BY channel ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Total balance of active accounts by branch region
gold_sql: SELECT b.region, SUM(a.current_balance) AS total_balance FROM accounts a JOIN branches b ON a.branch_id = b.branch_id WHERE a.status = 'active' GROUP BY b.region ORDER BY total_balance DESC;

question: Number of accounts per branch city
gold_sql: SELECT b.city, COUNT(a.account_id) AS n FROM branches b JOIN accounts a ON a.branch_id = b.branch_id GROUP BY b.city ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Average customer income by state of residence (top 10)
gold_sql: SELECT state, ROUND(AVG(annual_income), 2) AS avg_income, COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers GROUP BY state ORDER BY avg_income DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Total deposit principal by product
gold_sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(d.principal) AS total_principal, COUNT(*) AS n_deposits FROM deposits d JOIN accounts a ON d.account_id = a.account_id JOIN products p ON a.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_principal DESC;

question: Average loan amount by product
gold_sql: SELECT p.name, ROUND(AVG(l.principal), 2) AS avg_principal, COUNT(*) AS n FROM loans l JOIN products p ON l.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY avg_principal DESC;

question: Number of customers per branch (by their accounts)
gold_sql: SELECT b.name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.customer_id) AS n_customers FROM branches b JOIN accounts a ON a.branch_id = b.branch_id GROUP BY b.name ORDER BY n_customers DESC;

question: Total credit limit issued by customer occupation
gold_sql: SELECT c.occupation, SUM(cc.credit_limit) AS total_limit FROM credit_cards cc JOIN customers c ON cc.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE cc.status = 'active' GROUP BY c.occupation ORDER BY total_limit DESC;

question: Total active loan principal by customer state
gold_sql: SELECT c.state, SUM(l.principal) AS total_principal FROM loans l JOIN customers c ON l.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE l.status = 'active' GROUP BY c.state ORDER BY total_principal DESC;

question: Number of employees by branch region
gold_sql: SELECT b.region, COUNT(*) AS n_employees FROM employees e JOIN branches b ON e.branch_id = b.branch_id GROUP BY b.region ORDER BY n_employees DESC;

question: Customers in Maharashtra with their account balances
gold_sql: SELECT c.full_name, SUM(a.current_balance) AS total_balance FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id WHERE c.state = 'Maharashtra' AND a.status = 'active' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.full_name ORDER BY total_balance DESC LIMIT 50;

question: Total successful transaction volume by branch region
gold_sql: SELECT b.region, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN branches b ON a.branch_id = b.branch_id WHERE t.status = 'success' GROUP BY b.region ORDER BY total_volume DESC;

question: Average transaction amount by spending category for debits
gold_sql: SELECT tc.name AS category, ROUND(AVG(t.amount), 2) AS avg_amount FROM transactions t JOIN transaction_categories tc ON t.category_id = tc.category_id WHERE t.txn_type = 'debit' AND t.status = 'success' GROUP BY tc.name ORDER BY avg_amount DESC;

question: Total transaction volume by customer occupation
gold_sql: SELECT c.occupation, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE t.status = 'success' GROUP BY c.occupation ORDER BY total_volume DESC;

question: Spending by category and channel
gold_sql: SELECT tc.name AS category, t.channel, SUM(t.amount) AS total FROM transactions t JOIN transaction_categories tc ON t.category_id = tc.category_id WHERE t.status = 'success' AND t.txn_type = 'debit' GROUP BY tc.name, t.channel ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 50;

question: Top 10 branches by total successful transaction volume
gold_sql: SELECT b.name, b.city, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN branches b ON a.branch_id = b.branch_id WHERE t.status = 'success' GROUP BY b.branch_id, b.name, b.city ORDER BY total_volume DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Customer count by income bucket and state
gold_sql: SELECT c.state, CASE WHEN c.annual_income < 300000 THEN 'low' WHEN c.annual_income < 1000000 THEN 'mid' ELSE 'high' END AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers c GROUP BY c.state, bucket ORDER BY c.state, bucket;

question: Loan portfolio by product type and status
gold_sql: SELECT p.product_type, l.status, SUM(l.principal) AS total_principal, COUNT(*) AS n FROM loans l JOIN products p ON l.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.product_type, l.status ORDER BY p.product_type, l.status;

question: FD principal by branch region
gold_sql: SELECT b.region, SUM(d.principal) AS total_principal FROM deposits d JOIN accounts a ON d.account_id = a.account_id JOIN branches b ON a.branch_id = b.branch_id WHERE d.status = 'active' GROUP BY b.region ORDER BY total_principal DESC;

question: Transactions count by channel and branch region
gold_sql: SELECT b.region, t.channel, COUNT(*) AS n FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN branches b ON a.branch_id = b.branch_id WHERE t.status = 'success' GROUP BY b.region, t.channel ORDER BY b.region, n DESC;

question: Number of campaigns each customer was contacted in
gold_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name, COUNT(DISTINCT cc.campaign_id) AS n_campaigns FROM customers c JOIN campaign_contacts cc ON c.customer_id = cc.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.full_name ORDER BY n_campaigns DESC LIMIT 20;

question: Customers with more than 3 active accounts
gold_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name, COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id WHERE a.status = 'active' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.full_name HAVING COUNT(*) > 3 ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Branches with total balance above 1 crore
gold_sql: SELECT b.name, SUM(a.current_balance) AS total FROM branches b JOIN accounts a ON a.branch_id = b.branch_id WHERE a.status = 'active' GROUP BY b.branch_id, b.name HAVING SUM(a.current_balance) > 10000000 ORDER BY total DESC;

question: Categories with more than 1000 transactions
gold_sql: SELECT tc.name, COUNT(*) AS n FROM transactions t JOIN transaction_categories tc ON t.category_id = tc.category_id GROUP BY tc.name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1000 ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Customers with total loan principal above 50 lakhs
gold_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name, SUM(l.principal) AS total FROM customers c JOIN loans l ON c.customer_id = l.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.full_name HAVING SUM(l.principal) > 5000000 ORDER BY total DESC;

question: Campaigns with more than 100 subscribed customers
gold_sql: SELECT c.campaign_id, c.name, SUM(CASE WHEN cc.subscribed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_sub FROM campaigns c JOIN campaign_contacts cc ON c.campaign_id = cc.campaign_id GROUP BY c.campaign_id, c.name HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN cc.subscribed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 100 ORDER BY n_sub DESC;

question: Failed transaction percentage by channel
gold_sql: SELECT channel, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'failed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS failed_pct FROM transactions GROUP BY channel ORDER BY failed_pct DESC;

question: Pending transaction percentage by channel
gold_sql: SELECT channel, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'pending' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS pending_pct FROM transactions GROUP BY channel ORDER BY pending_pct DESC;

question: Loan default rate by product type
gold_sql: SELECT p.product_type, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN l.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS default_pct FROM loans l JOIN products p ON l.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.product_type ORDER BY default_pct DESC;

question: Account dormancy rate by branch region
gold_sql: SELECT b.region, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN a.status = 'dormant' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS dormant_pct FROM accounts a JOIN branches b ON a.branch_id = b.branch_id GROUP BY b.region ORDER BY dormant_pct DESC;

question: Subscription rate by campaign channel
gold_sql: SELECT c.channel, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN cc.subscribed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS conversion_pct FROM campaigns c JOIN campaign_contacts cc ON c.campaign_id = cc.campaign_id GROUP BY c.channel ORDER BY conversion_pct DESC;

question: Share of total transaction volume by channel
gold_sql: SELECT channel, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(amount) / SUM(SUM(amount)) OVER (), 2) AS pct_volume FROM transactions WHERE status = 'success' GROUP BY channel ORDER BY pct_volume DESC;

question: Default flag percentage by customer occupation
gold_sql: SELECT occupation, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN has_default THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS default_pct FROM customers GROUP BY occupation ORDER BY default_pct DESC;

question: Customers who have at least one credit card
gold_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name FROM customers c WHERE c.customer_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM credit_cards) ORDER BY c.customer_id LIMIT 100;

question: Customers without any active loan
gold_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name FROM customers c WHERE c.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM loans WHERE status = 'active') ORDER BY c.customer_id LIMIT 100;

question: Customers who have both a deposit and a credit card
gold_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name FROM customers c WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM deposits d WHERE d.customer_id = c.customer_id) AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM credit_cards cc WHERE cc.customer_id = c.customer_id) LIMIT 100;

question: Customers contacted in campaigns but never subscribed
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.customer_id, c.full_name FROM customers c JOIN campaign_contacts cc ON c.customer_id = cc.customer_id WHERE c.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM campaign_contacts WHERE subscribed = TRUE) LIMIT 100;

question: Branches with no active loans booked from customers in their state
gold_sql: SELECT b.branch_id, b.name FROM branches b WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM accounts a JOIN loans l ON a.customer_id = l.customer_id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE a.branch_id = b.branch_id AND l.status = 'active' AND c.state = b.state);

question: Customers with at least one defaulted loan
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.customer_id, c.full_name FROM customers c WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM loans l WHERE l.customer_id = c.customer_id AND l.status = 'defaulted') ORDER BY c.customer_id;

question: Customers holding all three: deposit account, loan, credit card (active)
gold_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name FROM customers c WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM accounts a WHERE a.customer_id = c.customer_id AND a.status = 'active') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM loans l WHERE l.customer_id = c.customer_id AND l.status = 'active') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM credit_cards cc WHERE cc.customer_id = c.customer_id AND cc.status = 'active');

question: Top 3 branches by total balance in each region
gold_sql: WITH br AS (SELECT b.region, b.branch_id, b.name, SUM(a.current_balance) AS total_bal FROM branches b JOIN accounts a ON b.branch_id = a.branch_id WHERE a.status = 'active' GROUP BY b.region, b.branch_id, b.name), ranked AS (SELECT *, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY total_bal DESC) AS rnk FROM br) SELECT region, name, total_bal FROM ranked WHERE rnk <= 3 ORDER BY region, rnk;

question: Cumulative monthly transaction volume in 2024
gold_sql: WITH monthly AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', txn_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS volume FROM transactions WHERE txn_date >= '2024-01-01' AND txn_date < '2025-01-01' AND status = 'success' GROUP BY 1) SELECT month, volume, SUM(volume) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS cumulative FROM monthly ORDER BY month;

question: Customers ranked within each occupation by annual income (top 3 each)
gold_sql: WITH ranked AS (SELECT customer_id, full_name, occupation, annual_income, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY occupation ORDER BY annual_income DESC) AS rnk FROM customers) SELECT occupation, full_name, annual_income FROM ranked WHERE rnk <= 3 ORDER BY occupation, rnk;

question: Income deciles for customers and their average account balance
gold_sql: WITH d AS (SELECT customer_id, NTILE(10) OVER (ORDER BY annual_income) AS decile FROM customers) SELECT d.decile, ROUND(AVG(a.current_balance), 2) AS avg_balance, COUNT(DISTINCT d.customer_id) AS n FROM d JOIN accounts a ON d.customer_id = a.customer_id WHERE a.status = 'active' GROUP BY d.decile ORDER BY d.decile;

question: Top 5 merchants by total transaction amount in each category
gold_sql: WITH m AS (SELECT tc.name AS category, t.merchant, SUM(t.amount) AS total FROM transactions t JOIN transaction_categories tc ON t.category_id = tc.category_id WHERE t.status = 'success' AND t.merchant IS NOT NULL GROUP BY tc.name, t.merchant), r AS (SELECT *, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY total DESC) AS rnk FROM m) SELECT category, merchant, total FROM r WHERE rnk <= 5 ORDER BY category, rnk;

question: Month-over-month growth in successful transaction count in 2024
gold_sql: WITH m AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', txn_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS n FROM transactions WHERE status = 'success' AND txn_date >= '2024-01-01' AND txn_date < '2025-01-01' GROUP BY 1) SELECT month, n, ROUND(100.0 * (n - LAG(n) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / NULLIF(LAG(n) OVER (ORDER BY month), 0), 2) AS mom_pct FROM m ORDER BY month;

question: Rolling 3-month average transaction volume
gold_sql: WITH m AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', txn_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS volume FROM transactions WHERE status = 'success' GROUP BY 1) SELECT month, volume, ROUND(AVG(volume) OVER (ORDER BY month ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW), 2) AS rolling_3m FROM m ORDER BY month;

question: Transactions in the last 30 days
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM transactions WHERE txn_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' AND status = 'success';

question: Customers onboarded in 2024
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers WHERE onboarded_at >= '2024-01-01' AND onboarded_at < '2025-01-01';

question: Monthly count of new customers in 2025
gold_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', onboarded_at) AS month, COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers WHERE onboarded_at >= '2025-01-01' AND onboarded_at < '2026-01-01' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

question: Total transaction volume in Q1 2025
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(amount) AS total_volume FROM transactions WHERE txn_date >= '2025-01-01' AND txn_date < '2025-04-01' AND status = 'success';

question: Loans disbursed in 2024 by month
gold_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', disbursed_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(principal) AS total FROM loans WHERE disbursed_date >= '2024-01-01' AND disbursed_date < '2025-01-01' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

question: Deposits maturing in the next 90 days
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(principal) AS total FROM deposits WHERE status = 'active' AND maturity_date BETWEEN CURRENT_DATE AND CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '90 days';

question: Conversion rate by campaign objective
gold_sql: SELECT c.objective, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN cc.subscribed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS conversion_pct, COUNT(*) AS n FROM campaigns c JOIN campaign_contacts cc ON c.campaign_id = cc.campaign_id GROUP BY c.objective ORDER BY conversion_pct DESC;

question: Best converting campaign by absolute subscribers
gold_sql: SELECT c.campaign_id, c.name, SUM(CASE WHEN cc.subscribed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_sub FROM campaigns c JOIN campaign_contacts cc ON c.campaign_id = cc.campaign_id GROUP BY c.campaign_id, c.name ORDER BY n_sub DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Subscription rate by target segment
gold_sql: SELECT c.target_segment, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN cc.subscribed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS conversion_pct FROM campaigns c JOIN campaign_contacts cc ON c.campaign_id = cc.campaign_id GROUP BY c.target_segment ORDER BY conversion_pct DESC;

question: Average contact attempts per customer for subscribed vs not_subscribed
gold_sql: SELECT outcome, ROUND(AVG(contact_count), 2) AS avg_contacts FROM campaign_contacts GROUP BY outcome ORDER BY avg_contacts DESC;

question: Customers contacted in 3 or more campaigns
gold_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name, COUNT(DISTINCT cc.campaign_id) AS n_camp FROM customers c JOIN campaign_contacts cc ON c.customer_id = cc.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.full_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT cc.campaign_id) >= 3 ORDER BY n_camp DESC LIMIT 50;

question: Active FD principal by term length
gold_sql: SELECT term_months, SUM(principal) AS total, COUNT(*) AS n FROM deposits WHERE status = 'active' GROUP BY term_months ORDER BY term_months;

question: Average interest rate on active deposits by term
gold_sql: SELECT term_months, ROUND(AVG(interest_rate), 2) AS avg_rate FROM deposits WHERE status = 'active' GROUP BY term_months ORDER BY term_months;

question: Top 10 customers by total FD principal
gold_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name, SUM(d.principal) AS total FROM customers c JOIN deposits d ON c.customer_id = d.customer_id WHERE d.status = 'active' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.full_name ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Number of matured FDs in 2024
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM deposits WHERE status = 'matured' AND maturity_date >= '2024-01-01' AND maturity_date < '2025-01-01';

question: Total active home loan principal
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(l.principal) AS total FROM loans l JOIN products p ON l.product_id = p.product_id WHERE l.status = 'active' AND p.product_type = 'home_loan';

question: Average EMI by loan product
gold_sql: SELECT p.name, ROUND(AVG(l.emi), 2) AS avg_emi FROM loans l JOIN products p ON l.product_id = p.product_id WHERE l.status IN ('active', 'overdue') GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY avg_emi DESC;

question: Overdue loans grouped by customer occupation
gold_sql: SELECT c.occupation, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(l.principal) AS total_principal FROM loans l JOIN customers c ON l.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE l.status = 'overdue' GROUP BY c.occupation ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Defaulted loans by customer state
gold_sql: SELECT c.state, COUNT(*) AS n FROM loans l JOIN customers c ON l.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE l.status = 'defaulted' GROUP BY c.state ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Average credit utilization across active cards
gold_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(current_balance / NULLIF(credit_limit, 0)) * 100, 2) AS avg_util_pct FROM credit_cards WHERE status = 'active';

question: Credit utilization by customer age bucket
gold_sql: SELECT CASE WHEN c.age < 30 THEN '<30' WHEN c.age < 45 THEN '30-44' WHEN c.age < 60 THEN '45-59' ELSE '60+' END AS bucket, ROUND(AVG(cc.current_balance / NULLIF(cc.credit_limit, 0)) * 100, 2) AS avg_util FROM credit_cards cc JOIN customers c ON cc.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE cc.status = 'active' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

question: Number of blocked credit cards
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM credit_cards WHERE status = 'blocked';

question: Total credit limit issued by branch region (via customer state mapping)
gold_sql: SELECT b.region, SUM(cc.credit_limit) AS total_limit FROM credit_cards cc JOIN customers c ON cc.customer_id = c.customer_id JOIN accounts a ON a.customer_id = c.customer_id JOIN branches b ON a.branch_id = b.branch_id WHERE cc.status = 'active' GROUP BY b.region ORDER BY total_limit DESC;

question: Top branch by assets under management (active deposit balance)
gold_sql: SELECT b.branch_id, b.name, b.city, SUM(a.current_balance) AS aum FROM branches b JOIN accounts a ON b.branch_id = a.branch_id WHERE a.status = 'active' GROUP BY b.branch_id, b.name, b.city ORDER BY aum DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Number of active accounts per branch
gold_sql: SELECT b.name, COUNT(*) AS n FROM branches b JOIN accounts a ON b.branch_id = a.branch_id WHERE a.status = 'active' GROUP BY b.branch_id, b.name ORDER BY n DESC;

question: Average customer balance per branch
gold_sql: SELECT b.name, ROUND(AVG(a.current_balance), 2) AS avg_balance FROM branches b JOIN accounts a ON b.branch_id = a.branch_id WHERE a.status = 'active' GROUP BY b.branch_id, b.name ORDER BY avg_balance DESC;

question: Branch employee count and average salary
gold_sql: SELECT b.name, COUNT(e.employee_id) AS n_emp, ROUND(AVG(e.salary), 2) AS avg_salary FROM branches b JOIN employees e ON e.branch_id = b.branch_id GROUP BY b.branch_id, b.name ORDER BY n_emp DESC;

question: Among home loan customers, percentage that also hold an FD
gold_sql: WITH hl AS (SELECT DISTINCT l.customer_id FROM loans l JOIN products p ON l.product_id = p.product_id WHERE p.product_type = 'home_loan'), fd AS (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM deposits) SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN fd.customer_id IS NOT NULL THEN hl.customer_id END) / COUNT(DISTINCT hl.customer_id), 2) AS pct, COUNT(DISTINCT hl.customer_id) AS n FROM hl LEFT JOIN fd ON hl.customer_id = fd.customer_id;

question: Average annual income comparison: customers with home loan vs without
gold_sql: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM loans l JOIN products p ON l.product_id = p.product_id WHERE l.customer_id = c.customer_id AND p.product_type = 'home_loan') THEN 'has_home_loan' ELSE 'no_home_loan' END AS segment, ROUND(AVG(annual_income), 2) AS avg_income, COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers c GROUP BY 1;
